reasoning: Currently, over 68% of Fortune 500 companies are incorporated in Delaware (around 340 companies). Dropping below 50% by the end of 2026 would require at least 90 of these massive companies to reincorporate elsewhere. While there has been news coverage of a "DExit" triggered by figures like Elon Musk and Bill Ackman, the actual volume of departures is very low. A late 2025 report indicated that practically across the entire public market, only a few dozen companies had initiated moves out of Delaware, with the vast majority of corporations remaining due to the predictability of the Delaware Chancery Court and the strong preference of institutional investors. Reincorporation requires lengthy board processes, proxy statements, and shareholder votes, making a sudden mass exodus of ~90 Fortune 500 companies in the remaining 8 months of 2026 a practical impossibility. Given the stringent constraints and recent data indicating the trend is extremely minor, the probability is near zero.
